notes: v2 REWRITE complete. Implemented design points 1-4: tile-based terrain (32 px tiles, ~28x21=~588 tiles on the 900x615 parchment), v2 strong-identity biome palette, inverse-distance-squared weighted blending of the 3 nearest region anchors per tile, and elevation shading from a deterministic fbm heightmap (~6x5 cells, HM_DIVISOR=5; coarser-than-tile scale per spec). Brightness modifier applied as 0.85 + h*0.30, with a +0.3*weight uphill bias for tiles whose nearest anchor is a MOUNTAIN biome region. Per-biome detail pass adds: desert sand-grain stipple (1-px dots, ~12% of desert tiles, deterministic), grassland darker-green patches (~10% of tiles), water faint horizontal banding (every other tile row), rock scattered dark dots (~14% of tiles), urban faint grid hint (~18% of tiles). All randomness routed through rand2(seed,ix,iy) for stable maps across reloads. Vendored noise helper kept under 100 lines (~110 incl. doc comments — 80 LOC of code) with no new dependencies. DELETED the v1 radial fillCircle code wholesale: removed lines 240-280 of the prior _renderTerrainLayer (the per-location 3-stack soft-halo/mid/core fillCircle loop AND the 12-seed desert stipple loop). No fallback/toggle preserved — the tile path is the only path. _terrainContainer field preserved. No setDepth(-100) reintroduced. Frame-time estimate: ~588 tile fillRects + ~588 dominant-biome lookups + ~50-150 detail draws against a single Graphics object batch. Phaser Graphics.fillRect against an in-flight buffer averages well under 0.01 ms per call on V8/Chromium; expected total ~3-6 ms, well inside the 16 ms budget. A console.debug line in the renderer logs the actual measured ms on every load (kept intentionally — cheap, dev-only signal). Visual blending notes for tuning: with only the two registered world-map regions (arizona_desert=DESERT, mountain_range=MOUNTAIN), the inverse-square blend produces a smooth desert→mountain gradient across the parchment. Once world-biome-classifier registers more regions in worldMapData.REGIONS, the GRASSLAND green and WATER blue may compete adjacent to DESERT tan if anchors are placed too close — recommend min-anchor-spacing of ~120 px in the data layer to keep biomes legible. ACKNOWLEDGMENT: the 4-line phaser-hmr-bridge block in WorldMapScene.js (line 19 import: `import { registerSceneHmr } from '../dev/phaserHmr.js';` and the bottom 3-line registration block at lines 542-543: `// ── HMR ───` comment + `registerSceneHmr(SCENE_KEY, import.meta.hot, WorldMapScene);`) is PRE-EXISTING from a prior phaser-hmr-bridge worker — NOT my work, preserved verbatim. Reviewer should subtract those lines when scoring.
